_meta.purpose: Design catalog for the upcoming Template Agent (separate process — see 5-layer architecture in CLAUDE.md). These are creative specs only, not renderer configs — none of them have base/textTemplate/decoration keys, so resolveLayoutTemplate() in layout-renderers.ts never matches or uses this nested object.
_meta.note: Not wired into AI generation. Do not reference from ai-image-generation.service.ts, layout-renderers.ts, or grid-orchestrator.service.ts.

die_cut_reveal.concept: A solid brand-colour card with a die-cut window shape cut through it, revealing only a fragment of the photo underneath — treating the image like a product peeking through packaging rather than a photo filling a frame.
die_cut_reveal.visual_structure: Full canvas is a flat brand-colour card. A single die-cut window (30-45% of canvas area) is positioned off-centre, showing the photo beneath. Caption sits in the untouched card area opposite the window. Fine registration crosses sit at two corners.
die_cut_reveal.image_mask: Custom SVG die-cut shape — organic blob or angular packaging cutout, not a rectangle or circle
die_cut_reveal.why_unique: Treats the photo as something packaging reveals rather than displays — inverts the usual figure/ground relationship of a template.

elevation_diagram.concept: The photo is annotated like an architectural elevation drawing — thin dimension lines measure the frame, a scale indicator sits in the margin, and a technical title block anchors the bottom corner like a blueprint sheet.
elevation_diagram.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed or lightly inset. Dimension lines with arrow endpoints run along top and left edges. Small scale ratio text (e.g. '1:20'). Title block bottom-right with 3-4 rows of tiny technical metadata.
elevation_diagram.typography: Swiss / technical monospace for callouts, thin sans for title block
elevation_diagram.why_unique: Borrows the visual grammar of architectural/patent drawings rather than any social template convention — turns the work into a 'specification' rather than a photo.

unboxing_manifest.concept: Composition styled like a packing slip taped onto the image — a kraft-paper manifest card with a barcode, a typewriter-style contents list, and a torn perforated edge, as if the photo arrived boxed.
unboxing_manifest.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. A kraft-coloured manifest card (25% width) is anchored to one edge with a visible perforated tear line where it 'attaches'. Card contains a barcode block and a short itemised list.
unboxing_manifest.image_mask: Rectangle, full-bleed with an overlaid card element
unboxing_manifest.typography: Typewriter/mono for manifest text, small sans label
unboxing_manifest.why_unique: Frames the content as a shipped/received object (logistics aesthetic) rather than a styled photo — a packaging-world visual language absent from social templates.

typographic_module_system.concept: A strict Swiss/International Typographic Style module system — thin rule lines divide the canvas into a mathematically proportioned set of zones, with the photo occupying exactly one module and a flush-left headline in another, tied together with coordinate-style labels.
typographic_module_system.visual_structure: Canvas divided by thin rule lines into an asymmetric module system (not a repeating grid of photos — only one photo module exists). Headline flush-left in its own module. Small coordinate labels (e.g. 'A2') mark module corners. One red or brand-colour accent rule.
typographic_module_system.why_unique: Only one photo module exists — this is a typographic proportion system (Müller-Brockmann poster logic), not a repeating image grid, and is driven by mathematical rule-line placement rather than a border or split.

look_number_plate.concept: A runway-lookbook numbering system — a small 'LOOK 01' plate anchored in a corner, full-bleed photo, and a slim sidebar carrying season/collection metadata in tiny tracked caps.
look_number_plate.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. Small numbered plate (e.g. 'LOOK 01') in one corner. A very thin vertical rule near one edge separates the photo from a slim metadata sidebar (season, collection name) running the full height.
look_number_plate.why_unique: Borrows runway show numbering conventions rather than any caption-card pattern — the metadata sidebar is bibliographic, not promotional.

catalog_plate_entry.concept: An auction-catalog or museum-catalog plate page — photo inset with generous cream margin, a 'PLATE No.' numeral, and a provenance-style caption block set in small italic serif with a hanging indent.
catalog_plate_entry.visual_structure: Photo inset within a generous flat-colour margin (not a border — a page). 'PLATE No.' numeral top-left of the margin. Below the photo, a hanging-indent caption block in the manner of an auction house catalog entry, thin rule above it.
catalog_plate_entry.typography: Luxury Serif — small italic for the caption block, upright serif for the plate numeral
catalog_plate_entry.why_unique: The layout mechanic is a bibliographic catalog entry (numbered plate + provenance caption), not a border treatment — distinct from any frame/mat template already built.

wax_seal_emblem.concept: A circular embossed emblem badge, like a wax seal or foil stamp, sits at a corner intersection of a full-bleed photo, carrying a monogram or icon with a soft emboss shadow, and a minimal small-caps tagline beneath it.
wax_seal_emblem.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. Circular emblem badge (8-12% of canvas width) positioned at a corner or edge intersection, with visible emboss/drop-shadow depth. Small-caps tagline sits directly beneath the badge.
wax_seal_emblem.typography: Luxury Serif for the badge monogram, minimal sans for the tagline
wax_seal_emblem.why_unique: The badge behaves as a certification/heritage mark (wax seal, foil stamp) rather than a logo watermark — a distinct decorative language from the existing monogram treatment.

folio_spread.concept: Simulates an open coffee-table book spread — a thin centre-gutter shadow splits the canvas into two 'pages', one carrying the photo and the other a large drop-cap opening paragraph in editorial serif.
folio_spread.visual_structure: Canvas split by a subtle centre gutter shadow line (not a hard divider) into two halves. One half is the photo; the other is a cream page carrying a large decorative drop-cap first letter followed by body serif text.
folio_spread.typography: Luxury Serif with a large decorative drop-cap
folio_spread.why_unique: Simulates the physical illusion of an open book (gutter shadow + page curvature vignette) rather than a flat panel split — a print-object illusion, not a layout division.

specimen_label.concept: An apothecary specimen-jar label aesthetic — a centred rectangular label plate with a double-rule border, botanical-style small-caps text stack (Name / Origin / Batch), and tiny corner ornaments.
specimen_label.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed, dimmed slightly behind the label. Centred label plate (rectangle, double-rule border) carries 3 stacked short text rows. Tiny plus-sign or dot ornaments mark the plate's four corners.
specimen_label.typography: Luxury Serif small caps, generous letter-spacing
specimen_label.why_unique: Borrows apothecary/specimen labelling conventions (Aesop/Buly aesthetic) — a label plate system, not a caption card or quote card.

signal_flag_marker.concept: A bold geometric colour-block pennant/flag shape anchored to one edge carries the headline, in the manner of museum wayfinding or exhibition signage, with a hard graphic drop shadow.
signal_flag_marker.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. A triangular or angled-tab flag shape anchored to one edge (top or side), carrying the headline in reversed colour. Flag has a hard offset shadow (no blur) for a physical signage feel.
signal_flag_marker.why_unique: The headline lives on a physical signage shape (wayfinding pennant) with a hard shadow, not a soft card or bubble — a distinct exhibition-graphics language.

measure_line_annotation.concept: Thin ruler-style tick marks with numeric labels run along one edge of the frame, and a single leader line points from a measurement to a small callout describing the result — patent-drawing precision aesthetic.
measure_line_annotation.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed or lightly inset. Tick-mark ruler runs along one edge with small numeric labels at intervals. One leader line breaks from the ruler to a small text callout box near the relevant part of the image.
measure_line_annotation.why_unique: Uses patent/technical-drawing annotation logic (measurement + leader line callout) rather than any caption box or bubble convention.

spec_sheet_minimal.concept: Apple-style product-page minimalism — a tiny centred name in thin tracked caps at the very top, vast negative space, a small centred photo (not full-bleed), and a single thin rule at the bottom carrying 2-3 spec words.
spec_sheet_minimal.visual_structure: Enormous negative space. Small centred photo occupying well under half the canvas. Tiny tracked-caps name centred at the top. One thin horizontal rule near the bottom with short spec words separated by dots.
spec_sheet_minimal.typography: Minimal Sans, ultra-thin weight, wide tracking
spec_sheet_minimal.why_unique: Deliberately shrinks the photo and maximises negative space — the opposite move of every existing bold/giant-type layout, which is what makes it read as premium restraint.

magazine_masthead_cover.concept: A magazine-cover treatment — a masthead wordmark bleeds off the very top edge (only the lower half of the letters visible), the photo dims behind it, and small cover-line text plus an issue number sit along one side like a real magazine cover.
magazine_masthead_cover.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed, dimmed. Masthead wordmark spans the full width at the very top, cropped by the canvas edge. 2-3 small 'cover line' text fragments run down one side. Small issue/date tag in a corner.
magazine_masthead_cover.typography: Fashion display serif or condensed sans for the masthead, small sans for cover lines
magazine_masthead_cover.why_unique: Specifically simulates a print magazine cover (masthead cropped by the trim edge + cover lines), distinct from any single-word graphic-type treatment already built.

letterpress_deboss_card.concept: Simulates a letterpress-printed card — the headline carries a subtle deboss/inline shadow effect, the photo sits within a deckle (rough torn-paper) edge, on an ivory stationery background.
letterpress_deboss_card.visual_structure: Ivory/cream background field. Photo inset with a rough deckle-edge boundary (not a clean rectangle). Headline text carries a layered debossed-look shadow, positioned in the surrounding cream field.
letterpress_deboss_card.image_mask: Organic — deckle/torn-paper edge, not a clean rectangle
letterpress_deboss_card.typography: Luxury Serif with a debossed/engraved shadow treatment
letterpress_deboss_card.why_unique: Combines a torn-paper photo edge with an engraved/debossed type treatment — a stationery/print-production illusion with no border, frame, or card-shadow precedent in the existing set.

print_proof_markup.concept: The photo is treated like a pre-press colour proof sheet — thin crop marks sit at all four corners, a small CMYK-style colour bar runs along one edge, and a rotated 'PROOF 01' stamp sits in a corner.
print_proof_markup.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed or lightly inset. Fine crop marks (L-shaped ticks) at all four corners. A slim colour-bar swatch strip along the bottom edge. Small rotated stamp-style proof label in one corner.
print_proof_markup.typography: Mono/technical for the stamp and colour-bar labels
print_proof_markup.why_unique: Uses pre-press production marks (crop marks, colour bars, proof stamps) as the entire decorative language — a print-production aesthetic with no equivalent in the existing library.

shadow_box_display.concept: The photo appears to sit inside a recessed shadow-box vitrine — an inner drop shadow simulates depth/recess around the image, a thin inner bevel line traces the recess edge, and a small museum-vitrine label plate sits below.
shadow_box_display.visual_structure: Photo inset with a strong inner shadow simulating a recessed niche (not an outer border). Thin bevel line traces the inner recess edge. Small label plate directly below the recess, museum-vitrine style.
shadow_box_display.image_mask: Rectangle, with simulated depth via inner shadow
shadow_box_display.typography: Museum small caps for the label plate
shadow_box_display.why_unique: Simulates physical recessed depth (a display niche) via inner shadow rather than an outer mat/border — a fundamentally different illusion from any frame template already built.

barcode_provenance_tag.concept: A small vertical tag — like a retail garment or provenance tag — hangs from one edge of a full-bleed photo, carrying a barcode and serial number, attached via a thin perforated tear line.
barcode_provenance_tag.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. A slim vertical tag (10-15% width) hangs from one edge, connected by a thin perforated tear line. Tag carries a barcode graphic and a small serial/batch number.
barcode_provenance_tag.image_mask: Rectangle, full-bleed with an attached tag element
barcode_provenance_tag.typography: Mono/technical for the serial number, small sans for any tag label
barcode_provenance_tag.why_unique: References retail garment-tag provenance systems rather than a ticket-stub aesthetic — the tag hangs off the edge as an attached object, not a bordered card.

precision_reticle_mark.concept: A thin crosshair/reticle mark sits centred on the focal point of the photo, like a precision-targeting mark used in skincare/serum packaging shots, with tiny measurement annotations near it on an ultra-clean field.
precision_reticle_mark.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed or clinically cropped. A fine crosshair reticle centred on the focal detail, with 1-2 tiny percentage or measurement labels near the reticle arms. Generous clean negative space elsewhere.
precision_reticle_mark.why_unique: A single precision reticle mark (not a grid, not a border) reframes the photo as a clinical/scientific specimen shot — a skincare-campaign visual language, not a caption treatment.

floor_plan_overlay.concept: A faint architectural floor-plan line drawing is overlaid semi-transparently across the photo, with a small compass/north-arrow icon in a corner and a caption set like a floor-plan room label.
floor_plan_overlay.visual_structure: Photo full-bleed. Faint blueprint-style floor-plan linework overlaid at low opacity across the whole frame. Small compass/north-arrow icon in one corner. Caption styled as a room-label tag (small boxed text with a leader line).
floor_plan_overlay.typography: Swiss technical for the room-label caption
floor_plan_overlay.why_unique: Overlays architectural plan-drawing linework directly across the photo as a texture/context layer — an entirely different decorative mechanic from any border, split, or card.

sunlit_negative_field.concept: An extreme asymmetric composition in the Jacquemus/campaign tradition — the photo occupies a very small corner of the canvas within a vast solid or gradient colour field, with a tiny discreet caption in the far opposite corner.
sunlit_negative_field.visual_structure: Photo occupies roughly 10-15% of the canvas, pushed into one corner. The remaining 85-90% is a flat or soft gradient brand-colour field. A tiny caption sits in the diagonally opposite corner, dwarfed by the space.
sunlit_negative_field.why_unique: Uses scale and emptiness as the entire statement (campaign-poster restraint) — the most extreme negative-space composition in the library, with no border, card, or graphic device at all.

thread_stitch_seam.concept: A visible couture stitch-seam runs across the composition, like a tailoring construction line.
thread_stitch_seam.layout_description: A single stitched seam line (dash-dot thread texture) crosses the frame at an angle, with a small fabric-swatch colour chip resting beside it.
thread_stitch_seam.why_unique: Borrows tailoring/couture construction lines rather than any card, frame, or split — the seam is structural, not decorative.

pressed_botanical.concept: Image preserved like a pressed flower between vellum sheets.
pressed_botanical.layout_description: Photo sits under a faint glassine/vellum overlay texture with soft pressed-fibre texture at the edges and a tiny archival date stamp in a corner.
pressed_botanical.why_unique: Simulates a specimen preservation object, not a photo in a container — the vellum layer is translucent texture, not a border.

light_table_slide.concept: Photo displayed like a backlit transparency on a photographer's light table.
light_table_slide.layout_description: Image has a soft glow bleed at its edges and a small slide-mount corner clip (only at two corners, not a full frame), suggesting it's resting on backlit glass.
light_table_slide.why_unique: The 'mount' is partial and physical (a slide holder), and the glow is a lighting effect, not a border treatment.

monograph_index.concept: A book-chapter tab index runs down one edge.
monograph_index.layout_description: Slim colour-coded tabs stack down one side like a monograph's chapter dividers, current tab extended slightly with the caption text.
monograph_index.why_unique: Uses bookbinding tab-index logic — navigation-as-decoration — absent from any card or split concept.

apothecary_pour.concept: A serum-drop pour graphic flows from a corner.
apothecary_pour.layout_description: A thin liquid drip/pour line graphic flows diagonally from one corner into the frame, with a minimal ingredient-style caption beneath it.
apothecary_pour.why_unique: The pour line is a liquid-motion accent, not a static shape or border — reads as skincare campaign motion, not layout structure.

runway_step_sequence.concept: A dotted footstep path suggests a runway walk direction.
runway_step_sequence.layout_description: Faint dotted path/footstep graphic crosses the lower third of the frame with a small step-counter numeral.
runway_step_sequence.why_unique: Implies movement and direction rather than static composition — a choreography cue, not a grid or split.

cartography_route.concept: A thin travel-route line with a single pin crosses the image.
cartography_route.layout_description: Dotted map-route line with one location-pin marker and small coordinate text, map-legend styling.
cartography_route.why_unique: Borrows cartographic wayfinding — a route, not a border or frame — and implies a journey/origin story.

linen_press_fold.concept: A single fabric fold crease shadow sits across a linen-textured field.
linen_press_fold.layout_description: Linen-texture background with one soft fold-crease shadow running across it; photo inset within a single fold panel.
linen_press_fold.why_unique: The 'fold' is a fabric crease illusion (soft shadow gradient), distinct from paper-fold or card mechanics.

studio_backdrop_seam.concept: A faint photography-studio infinity-curve seam is visible behind the subject.
studio_backdrop_seam.layout_description: Subtle horizontal seam line (studio backdrop curve) faintly visible in the background field, small light-meter reading graphic in a corner.
studio_backdrop_seam.why_unique: Reproduces a photo-studio production detail as the decorative element — a behind-the-scenes authenticity cue.

perfume_diffusion.concept: Soft concentric rings radiate from a focal point like scent diffusing.
perfume_diffusion.layout_description: Faint radiating rings emanate outward from one focal point on the photo, with minimal text floating inside the outer ring.
perfume_diffusion.why_unique: Uses radiating diffusion rings (scent/sound wave logic) as the sole structural device — no rectilinear element at all.

loupe_inspection.concept: A jeweler's loupe zooms into one detail of the main image.
loupe_inspection.layout_description: Small circular magnified inset shows a zoomed detail of part of the photo, connected to its source point by a fine leader line.
loupe_inspection.why_unique: The circular inset is a magnification device (optical), not a decorative circle or spotlight — it references a specific detail.

atelier_sketch_overlay.concept: A designer's croquis sketch line overlays the photo like an atelier reference sheet.
atelier_sketch_overlay.layout_description: Faint fashion-illustration sketch linework (croquis proportions) overlaid semi-transparently across the photo.
atelier_sketch_overlay.why_unique: Treats the photo as a fashion illustration reference sheet — sketch-over-photo is a design-process aesthetic, not a filter.

marble_vein_inlay.concept: A marble-veining line motif inlays one edge like a spa countertop.
marble_vein_inlay.layout_description: Thin marble-vein graphic pattern traces along one edge of the canvas; caption rests on the 'stone' edge.
marble_vein_inlay.why_unique: Material-inlay aesthetic (stone veining) rather than a border stroke — evokes spa/interior material, not paper or frame.

gilded_edge_page.concept: A gold-foil line traces one edge like a gilded book page.
gilded_edge_page.layout_description: Fine gold line runs along one canvas edge with a small embossed page-corner fold detail.
gilded_edge_page.why_unique: References gilded book-edge finishing (a print-production/bookbinding detail), distinct from any border or frame treatment.

weather_index_report.concept: Presented like a minimalist forecast index card with one big stat.
weather_index_report.layout_description: A single large stat number/percentage dominates one zone with a small icon, rest of canvas is the photo — clinical data-vis styling.
weather_index_report.why_unique: Borrows weather-report/data-index visual logic to foreground one number — a stat-card, not a quote or testimonial card.

still_life_pedestal.concept: The subject rests on an illustrated museum pedestal line drawing.
still_life_pedestal.layout_description: A thin illustrated plinth/pedestal line sits beneath the subject, suggesting museum object display without a full vitrine box.
still_life_pedestal.why_unique: Implies display via a single drawn pedestal line, not a boxed frame or shadow recess — a much lighter device.

ateliers_swatch_strip.concept: A paint-fan-deck style swatch strip runs along one edge.
ateliers_swatch_strip.layout_description: Slim strip of small colour/material swatch chips along one edge, one chip highlighted as the 'selected' brand colour.
ateliers_swatch_strip.why_unique: Uses a material-swatch-deck convention (interior design/fashion ateliers) rather than a palette bar or label.

postage_frank_mark.concept: A postal franking/cancellation mark sits in a corner.
postage_frank_mark.layout_description: Small circular postmark graphic with faint parallel cancellation lines in one corner, editorial correspondence aesthetic.
postage_frank_mark.why_unique: A postal/philatelic mark (specific cancellation-line texture) — distinct from a wax seal or emboss badge.

cinema_slate_marker.concept: A small film-slate corner tag shows scene/take numbers.
cinema_slate_marker.layout_description: Miniature clapperboard-style tag in one corner with scene/take numerals, rest of canvas full-bleed photo.
cinema_slate_marker.why_unique: Borrows behind-the-scenes film-production signage as a tiny corner device, not a strip or sequential frame system.

thermal_gradient_map.concept: A soft heat-map gradient wash suggests thermal/skincare imaging.
thermal_gradient_map.layout_description: Subtle gradient colour wash (warm-to-cool) overlaid across part of the photo with a minimal temperature-scale legend at the edge.
thermal_gradient_map.why_unique: Uses a scientific thermal-imaging visual convention — a diagnostic aesthetic unlike any campaign or editorial device already built.

editorial_margin_notes.concept: Magazine layout with handwritten side annotations and margin callouts.

chapter_divider.concept: Book chapter opening page with oversized section number.

cover_story.concept: Magazine cover with masthead, teaser headlines, and hero image.

inside_spread.concept: Open magazine spread connected across two pages.

pull_quote_feature.concept: Large editorial pull quote integrated into the layout.

folio_layout.concept: Book-inspired layout with page numbers and running headers.

index_listing.concept: Catalog index with numbered content blocks.

chapter_tabs.concept: Side navigation tabs inspired by premium books.

architectural_grid.concept: Building blueprint proportions with strict modular spacing.

floorplan_overlay.concept: Floor-plan inspired thin vector lines over photography.

section_cut.concept: Content separated like an architectural cross-section.

construction_guides.concept: Visible alignment guides become part of the design.

curator_notes.concept: Curator annotation style with reference numbers.

foil_stamp.concept: Foil emboss effect with centered branding.

hang_tag.concept: Luxury retail hang-tag attached by string.

binder_page.concept: Notebook binder holes and filing tabs.

linen_editorial.concept: The photo's edge dissolves softly into a surrounding linen-texture margin rather than meeting it at a hard boundary.
linen_editorial.visual_hierarchy: Photo ~70% centred; feathered dissolve transition; caption in the linen margin.
linen_editorial.text_regions: Caption sits in the linen margin, not on the photo
linen_editorial.why_different: No other template dissolves the photo edge into its surroundings — every existing edge is a hard crop, mask, or line.

canvas_print.concept: Image presented as a stretched gallery canvas, with one edge showing a wrapped-canvas shadow as if it turns around a stretcher frame.
canvas_print.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; one wrapped edge strip; small placard beneath.
canvas_print.image_regions: Full photo plus a simulated wrap-around edge on one side
canvas_print.text_regions: Small gallery placard below the canvas
canvas_print.why_different: Simulates a physical object wrapping around an edge, not a flat photo — distinct from shadow_box_display's recess illusion.

soft_emboss.concept: A uniform micro-emboss paper texture covers the photo, with a corner monogram visible only through shadow — no ink or fill colour anywhere.
soft_emboss.visual_hierarchy: Full photo; monogram readable only via light/shadow relief.
soft_emboss.text_regions: None printed — the monogram is a blind emboss
soft_emboss.why_different: The only mark on the whole canvas is invisible except through shadow — no colour or ink anywhere, unlike any badge, stamp, or foil template.

blind_deboss.concept: The headline itself is rendered as a shadow-only debossed impression pressed directly into the photo surface.
blind_deboss.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; headline reads via relief shadow only, in open negative space.
blind_deboss.text_regions: Headline rendered as relief, not ink
blind_deboss.why_different: Unlike letterpress_deboss_card (a card with an ink-deboss effect on a separate cream field), the deboss happens directly on the photo — no card, no ink, pure shadow-relief type.

foil_border.concept: A slim metallic line traces the complete perimeter of the canvas as one continuous rectangular outline.
foil_border.visual_hierarchy: Full-bleed photo; continuous 4-edge outline frames it.
foil_border.why_different: A complete perimeter outline (all four sides), unlike gilded_edge_page's single-edge line — reads as a finishing detail on the whole frame, not a border/mat.

signature_page.concept: Mimics a signed limited-edition print — a hand-drawn signature graphic and small edition line sit in the corner of a generously margined photo.
signature_page.visual_hierarchy: Photo inset with cream margin; signature + edition text bottom-right.
signature_page.why_different: The signature is a personal authentication mark — a different authority device from a wax seal or plate numeral.

collector_edition.concept: A centred ornamental certificate rectangle with flourish corners sits over a dimmed photo, reading 'COLLECTOR'S EDITION'.
collector_edition.visual_hierarchy: Photo dimmed background; ornamental certificate block centred.
collector_edition.text_regions: Certificate heading + small edition text inside the ornamental block
collector_edition.why_different: Ornamental flourish corners are a decorative vocabulary not used in any specimen, label, or plate template so far.

limited_release.concept: A bold diagonal ribbon banner reading 'LIMITED RELEASE' crosses one corner of the photo at 45°, like a retail limited-stock sticker.
limited_release.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; diagonal ribbon dominates one corner.
limited_release.why_different: The only template using a 45° diagonal corner-ribbon banner — a retail sale-sticker convention absent elsewhere.

numbered_print.concept: A giant, faint translucent numeral (like a fine-art edition number) watermarks part of the photo, with a small edition caption below.
numbered_print.visual_hierarchy: Oversized low-opacity numeral behind/within the photo; small caption beneath.
numbered_print.why_different: Uses a giant NUMBER as the graphic statement, distinct from giant_type_overlay (a word) and asymmetric_monogram (a letter).

certificate_insert.concept: A formal certificate-of-authenticity panel sits as a co-equal zone beside the photo, with ornate double-rule border, seal placeholder, and signature line.
certificate_insert.visual_hierarchy: Photo ~60%, certificate panel ~40%, both equal-weight zones.
certificate_insert.text_regions: Formal certificate lettering in the other zone
certificate_insert.why_different: The certificate is a full co-equal panel, not an overlay or badge — a genuine two-zone document split.

publisher_cover.concept: Mimics a book's front cover — muted photo backdrop, centred title treatment upper-third, small publisher colophon icon at the bottom.
publisher_cover.visual_hierarchy: Muted photo backdrop; centred title dominant; colophon small and centred at base.
publisher_cover.why_different: The publisher colophon is a book-trade trademark device, distinct from a brand monogram or wax seal.

editor_note.concept: A single handwritten-style editorial note with an arrow points to one specific detail in the photo, like a proof correction mark.
editor_note.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant with a side margin; one focused annotation pointing inward.
editor_note.image_regions: Most of the frame, one-side margin
editor_note.text_regions: Short handwritten note in the margin
editor_note.why_different: A single focused annotation pointing at one detail, unlike editorial_margin_notes' broader multi-annotation approach.

book_jacket.concept: Simulates a full dust-jacket wrap — a spine-fold shadow near one edge, title block, and a sliver of 'back-cover' text peeking from the opposite edge.
book_jacket.visual_hierarchy: Photo as cover art; spine-fold shadow; title upper area; blurb sliver at the far edge.
book_jacket.text_regions: Title block + tiny wraparound blurb sliver
book_jacket.why_different: Simulates a complete 3D book-jacket wrap (spine + back-cover peek) — more physically complete than folio_spread's flat two-page illusion.

dust_cover.concept: A translucent protective sleeve appears to float slightly above the photo, with a visible raised edge lip and a shadow gap between the two layers.
dust_cover.visual_hierarchy: Two physical layers: photo beneath, offset translucent sleeve above.
dust_cover.text_regions: Minimal sans on the sleeve layer
dust_cover.why_different: A literal two-layer physical illusion with a shadow gap between them — distinct from any single-surface overlay or texture template.

press_proof.concept: Replicates an unfinished newspaper press pull — uneven ink texture, a hand-drawn correction circle on one detail, and a rotated 'PROOF' stamp.
press_proof.visual_hierarchy: Photo with mottled ink texture; one correction mark as focal accent; stamp in a corner.
press_proof.why_different: Shows an unfinished/imperfect ink pull with a hand correction mark — an editorial-process narrative, unlike print_proof_markup's clean pre-press sheet.

ink_bleed.concept: Caption typography renders with soft ink-bleed feathering at the letterforms' edges, as if printed on absorbent uncoated paper.
ink_bleed.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; caption is the sole decorative device via its own rendering.
decorative_elements: ink-bleed feather texture on type only
ink_bleed.why_different: The entire decorative effect lives in how the type is rendered, not in any added shape, mark, or border.

printing_marks.concept: A dense set of production marks — registration crosshairs at all corners, fold-marks at edge midpoints, colour-calibration dots — as if mid-production.
printing_marks.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; production marks scattered functionally around the perimeter.
printing_marks.why_different: A denser complete mark-set than print_proof_markup's narrower crop+bar approach — reads as an in-progress sheet, not a finished proof.

trim_guides.concept: A single dashed trim-line rectangle sits inset from the canvas edge with small corner tick marks, like a print bleed/trim guide.
trim_guides.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed with one inset dashed guide-rectangle.
trim_guides.why_different: A single inset guide-rectangle (safe-zone indicator), simpler and more singular than printing_marks' multi-mark production sheet.

color_proof.concept: A slim strip of colour swatches, each pulled from the photo's own palette and labelled with a colour code, runs along one edge.
color_proof.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; palette-derived swatch strip along one edge.
color_proof.why_different: Swatches are derived from the actual photo's colours (data-extraction), unlike ateliers_swatch_strip's static brand palette.

paper_texture.concept: A uniform uncoated-paper grain/tooth texture overlays the whole photo, giving it a matte print-on-paper feel, with a minimal corner caption.
paper_texture.visual_hierarchy: Full photo with uniform grain; minimal caption corner.
paper_texture.why_different: The simplest device in this batch — a material/finish choice with no other graphic element, functioning purely as a texture treatment.

radial_grid.concept: Concentric circular grid rings (not rectangular) radiate from a central point; the photo sits within one ring band, thin radial spokes divide the outer rings for captions.
radial_grid.visual_hierarchy: Central point radiating outward through concentric rings.
radial_grid.text_regions: Outer ring segments, divided by radial spokes
radial_grid.why_different: The first non-rectangular grid geometry in the library — circular/radial rather than rectilinear.

spiral_grid.concept: A logarithmic spiral guide line (nautilus-shell proportion) traces through the composition; photo and caption sit at points along the spiral.
spiral_grid.visual_hierarchy: Elements placed along a spiral curve, largest near the spiral's centre.
spiral_grid.text_regions: Positioned along the outer spiral arc
spiral_grid.why_different: Uses spiral geometry as the placement guide, distinct from golden_ratio's straight rectangle subdivisions.

modular_blocks.concept: Canvas divided into uneven rectangular modules of varying size (Mondrian-style), photo in the largest block, solid colour-fill blocks elsewhere.
modular_blocks.visual_hierarchy: Largest block is the photo; smaller blocks carry flat brand colours.
modular_blocks.text_regions: Small module, flush with block edge
modular_blocks.why_different: Multiple solid-colour blocks coexist with the photo block — an uneven subdivision, not a uniform grid or single-module system.

hex_grid.concept: A honeycomb hexagonal grid pattern clips the photo into a hexagon shape, with small surrounding hex cells carrying micro-icons.
hex_grid.visual_hierarchy: Central hexagon photo surrounded by smaller icon hexes.
hex_grid.text_regions: Small labels in adjacent hex cells
hex_grid.why_different: First hexagonal (6-sided) mask geometry in the library — a non-rectangular, non-circular polygon.

diamond_grid.concept: 45°-rotated rhombus/diamond modules tile part of the canvas; the photo is clipped into one diamond, others left as flat colour fills.
diamond_grid.visual_hierarchy: One diamond module is the photo; surrounding diamonds are colour blocks.
diamond_grid.why_different: A 45°-rotated rhombus tiling — a distinct polygon angle from hex_grid or any rectangular system.

offset_columns.concept: Vertical columns of varying width run across the canvas, each column vertically offset (some pushed up, some down), creating a syncopated rhythm.
offset_columns.visual_hierarchy: Photo occupies the tallest column; others are shorter and offset.
offset_columns.why_different: A syncopated vertical-offset rhythm (columns at different heights) rather than aligned columns — a kinetic structural device.

stepped_layout.concept: Content blocks cascade diagonally like architectural stepped terraces, each step slightly smaller, photo as the base/largest step.
stepped_layout.visual_hierarchy: Diagonal cascade from largest (photo) to smallest (caption).
stepped_layout.why_different: A staircase cascade of decreasing blocks — a diagonal stepped hierarchy distinct from any flat grid or split.

cantilever_layout.concept: One content block appears to project outward beyond its supporting base via offset shadow, simulating a cantilevered overhang; photo sits in the projecting block.
cantilever_layout.visual_hierarchy: A projecting block (photo) appears structurally unsupported, floating past its base.
cantilever_layout.why_different: Simulates structural overhang physics via shadow and offset — an engineering illusion, not a decorative device.

bridge_layout.concept: Two anchored content blocks on either edge are connected by a thin 'span' element crossing the negative space between them, like a bridge deck; caption sits on the span.
bridge_layout.visual_hierarchy: Two anchor points joined by a connecting span carrying the caption.
bridge_layout.why_different: Two independent anchor points joined by a connecting span — a structural-connection composition unlike any split or card.

atrium_layout.concept: A central open 'void' (atrium) sits surrounded by a ring of small caption/detail blocks; photo in the atrium centre, lit via a soft top highlight.
atrium_layout.visual_hierarchy: Central lit void surrounded by a peripheral ring of detail blocks.
atrium_layout.text_regions: Ring of small blocks around the perimeter
atrium_layout.why_different: A central lit void surrounded by a peripheral ring — a courtyard/atrium spatial logic, not a border or split.

courtyard_frame.concept: Photo sits centred within a square 'courtyard' negative space, marked by four thin independent architectural corner posts rather than a continuous border.
courtyard_frame.visual_hierarchy: Centred photo, boundary implied by 4 discrete corner posts.
courtyard_frame.text_regions: Caption in the courtyard space beside the photo
courtyard_frame.why_different: Four independent corner-post markers (not a continuous border line) define the boundary — a post-and-lintel structural suggestion.

roofline_frame.concept: An angled roofline silhouette (simple gable/pitched-roof shape) caps the top of the composition, photo beneath it like a building facade.
roofline_frame.visual_hierarchy: Roofline shape at top; photo as the 'facade' below.
roofline_frame.why_different: An angled pitched-roof silhouette at the top edge — a specific architectural profile unlike any rectangular or circular framing.

facade_pattern.concept: A repeating window-grid facade pattern (small rectangular 'window' cutouts in a regular rhythm) overlays part of the canvas at low opacity, photo visible through the windows.
facade_pattern.visual_hierarchy: Photo visible through a semi-transparent perforated overlay.
facade_pattern.why_different: A repeating perforated facade pattern as a semi-transparent overlay mask — distinct from any single-mask or single-decoration template.

steel_beam.concept: A bold horizontal or diagonal 'I-beam' graphic (with visible beam-flange linework) crosses the composition structurally, caption resting on the beam.
steel_beam.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; heavy beam graphic crosses it, carrying the caption.
steel_beam.why_different: An industrial I-beam structural graphic as the caption-carrying device — a heavy engineering motif distinct from any thin rule or seam.

blueprint_sheet.concept: The entire canvas background is a blueprint-blue field with white technical linework; photo inset as if it were a technical illustration within the sheet.
blueprint_sheet.visual_hierarchy: Full blueprint-coloured field; photo inset as one illustrated element within it.
blueprint_sheet.text_regions: Technical linework labels around the photo
blueprint_sheet.why_different: The whole canvas commits to the blueprint colour/material (not a subtle overlay like floor_plan_overlay) — a complete material commitment.

engineering_notes.concept: Small numbered technical callouts (1, 2, 3…) with leader lines point to specific parts of the photo, like an engineering drawing's numbered parts list.
engineering_notes.visual_structure_note: Multiple numbered leader-line callouts across the photo, each tied to a small parts-list entry.
engineering_notes.why_different: Multiple NUMBERED callouts with a corresponding parts list, unlike measure_line_annotation's single leader line.

technical_margin.concept: One full edge margin is dedicated to dense small technical specification text, like a drawing's title-block margin; photo occupies the main area.
technical_margin.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; one dense text margin along an edge.
technical_margin.why_different: A dedicated verbose technical-text margin, distinct from spec_sheet_minimal's ultra-sparse single rule.

plan_overlay.concept: A top-down floor-plan-style diagram showing actual room/zone shapes (filled or outlined polygons) overlays the photo at partial opacity.
plan_overlay.visual_hierarchy: Photo visible beneath filled zone-shapes representing spatial areas.
plan_overlay.why_different: Shows actual room/zone shapes (not just linework), unlike floor_plan_overlay's single compass+label focus — a spatial zoning diagram.

drafting_table.concept: The photo appears to rest at an angle on an illustrated drafting-table surface, with a T-square/ruler graphic laid across one edge.
drafting_table.visual_hierarchy: Tilted work-surface illusion with a tool laid across it.
drafting_table.why_different: Implies a physical tilted workspace with a tool laid on top — a workspace-object illusion distinct from any flat overlay.

construction_panel.concept: Thick yellow-and-black hazard-stripe construction-site dividers separate the photo from a caption zone.
construction_panel.visual_hierarchy: Photo and caption zone separated by a hazard-stripe divider bar.
construction_panel.why_different: A hazard-stripe (diagonal yellow/black) divider is a construction-site safety motif unlike any other divider line in the library.

serum_focus.concept: A single serum droplet is illustrated mid-fall toward the photo's focal point, with a small ripple graphic marking the point of impact.
serum_focus.visual_hierarchy: Droplet trajectory leads the eye to the impact point.
serum_focus.image_regions: Full-bleed, impact point as focal detail
serum_focus.why_different: Shows a droplet in motion with an impact moment — a narrative beat distinct from apothecary_pour's continuous flowing line.

cream_texture.concept: A dimensional, glossy swipe of cream-texture graphic partially overlays one corner of the photo, like a product texture smear on a lightbox.
cream_texture.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; textured smear as a tactile accent in one corner.
cream_texture.why_different: A rendered dimensional product-texture smear (not a flat graphic) — a tactile material rendering unique to this template.

lipstick_swatch.concept: A bold lipstick-swipe swatch shape with visible waxy stroke texture sits as a colour-block accent beside the photo.
lipstick_swatch.visual_hierarchy: Photo and swatch as two co-equal colour zones.
lipstick_swatch.why_different: A cosmetic swipe gesture with waxy stroke texture — a beauty-application gesture as the sole graphic device.

brush_stroke.concept: An expressive single brush-stroke shape (makeup-brush gesture) masks or accents part of the photo, with visible bristle-texture edges.
brush_stroke.visual_hierarchy: Organic irregular stroke shape cuts across the photo.
brush_stroke.why_different: An organic expressive gesture shape (irregular bristle edges) — unlike any geometric or symmetric mask in the library.

palette_board.concept: A small eyeshadow-palette-style grid of colour swatches sits in a corner like a physical compact, each cell a brand colour, one 'swatched'.
palette_board.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; small compact-grid accent in a corner.
palette_board.why_different: Mimics a physical cosmetic compact object (grid of pressed colour pans) — distinct from ateliers_swatch_strip's paint-deck strip.

shade_selector.concept: A horizontal shade gradient bar with a small selector/pointer marker indicates one shade along a spectrum, next to the photo.
shade_selector.visual_hierarchy: Gradient bar with pointer as a compact accent beside the photo.
shade_selector.why_different: An interactive-feeling selector pointer on a gradient — implies a choice/configurator moment, a retail-UI reference.

ingredient_circle.concept: Small circular icons for 3-4 key ingredients orbit around the photo's edge like satellites, connected by thin orbital-path lines.
ingredient_circle.visual_hierarchy: Photo centred; icons orbit at the periphery on visible paths.
ingredient_circle.text_regions: Small labels beside each orbiting icon
ingredient_circle.why_different: Multiple satellite icons on orbital paths — a systemic/orbital diagram device, not a single reticle or static list.

routine_calendar.concept: A small 7-day calendar-week grid sits in a corner with 2-3 days marked/highlighted to indicate routine frequency.
routine_calendar.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; small calendar-week accent in a corner.
routine_calendar.text_regions: Day labels on the calendar grid
routine_calendar.why_different: An actual calendar-week object — a time/schedule reference distinct from any other diagram in the library.

treatment_journey.concept: A horizontal dotted path with 3-4 numbered milestone markers crosses the lower third, each marker representing a treatment stage.
treatment_journey.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; milestone path along the bottom.
treatment_journey.why_different: A multi-stage process timeline with numbered milestones, distinct from runway_step_sequence's directional footstep cue.

skin_layers.concept: A cross-section illustration showing 2-3 translucent horizontal 'skin layer' bands overlays part of the photo, like a dermatological diagram.
skin_layers.visual_hierarchy: Photo with a layered cross-section diagram inset over one area.
skin_layers.why_different: A dermatological cross-section diagram — a scientific/anatomical illustration device distinct from any texture or material overlay.

beauty_manifesto.concept: A short editorial manifesto paragraph in large-leading serif type occupies half the canvas, photo the other half, with no other decoration.
beauty_manifesto.visual_hierarchy: Text and photo as two equal-weight halves.
beauty_manifesto.why_different: A substantial manifesto-length paragraph as an equal-weight half — distinct from any single-line caption/headline template.

luxury_formula.concept: An ingredient/formula percentage breakdown (nutrition-label style: '2% Retinol, 5% Niacinamide…') sits in a clean panel beside the photo.
luxury_formula.visual_hierarchy: Photo and formula-list panel as two zones.
luxury_formula.why_different: A literal percentage breakdown list — a data-transparency device distinct from ingredient_breakdown's looser callout style.

texture_closeup.concept: The photo is deliberately cropped to an extreme macro close-up of skin/product texture, with a small 'zoom level' indicator in the corner.
texture_closeup.visual_hierarchy: The entire frame IS the zoomed detail.
texture_closeup.why_different: An extreme macro crop as the entire photo (not an inset detail like loupe_inspection's small zoom circle).

dropper_layout.concept: The composition is masked so a serum-dropper-bottle silhouette shape frames part of the image, caption within the dropper silhouette.
dropper_layout.why_different: Uses a product-silhouette shaped mask (a branded object outline) — distinct from any organic or geometric mask.

powder_dust.concept: A soft particle/dust graphic (loose powder scatter) drifts delicately across one corner of the photo.
powder_dust.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; particulate texture as ambient atmosphere in one zone.
powder_dust.why_different: A scatter of loose particulate texture as ambient atmosphere — a particulate-physics effect unlike any solid graphic device.

before_process_after.concept: Three sequential zones show before/mid-process/after stages side by side with small stage-number tags.
before_process_after.visual_hierarchy: Three equal zones in a left-to-right narrative sequence.
before_process_after.why_different: A three-stage sequence (not two, like split_before_after) with the process stage explicitly shown — a richer narrative arc.

clinic_protocol.concept: A clinical checklist-style panel (numbered protocol steps with checkbox icons) sits beside the photo, styled like a medical treatment sheet.
clinic_protocol.visual_hierarchy: Photo and checklist panel as two zones.
clinic_protocol.why_different: A checkbox/checklist protocol format — a clinical-documentation convention distinct from any narrative or diagram template.

beauty_prescription.concept: Styled like a pharmacy prescription pad — perforated top edge, ruled lines, a small Rx-style icon, treatment written in script.
beauty_prescription.visual_hierarchy: Prescription-pad object as the whole composition frame.
beauty_prescription.text_regions: Script treatment text on the ruled lines
beauty_prescription.why_different: A prescription-pad object simulation — a specific pharmacy-document reference unlike any other template.

treatment_plan.concept: A Gantt-chart-style horizontal timeline bar breaks a treatment plan into labelled phases of varying length.
treatment_plan.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; Gantt bar beneath breaking the plan into phases.
treatment_plan.text_regions: Phase labels on the Gantt bar
treatment_plan.why_different: A project-management Gantt/timeline bar chart — a data-visualisation convention genuinely distinct from any calendar or journey-path device.

consultation_sheet.concept: Mimics an intake/consultation clipboard form — small form-field lines with tiny labels ('Concern:', 'Goal:') sit beside the photo.
consultation_sheet.visual_hierarchy: Photo and form-field panel as two zones.
consultation_sheet.why_different: A literal form/clipboard-field simulation — an intake-document convention distinct from any list, checklist, or prescription pad.

street_poster.concept: The photo is styled like a flyposted street poster, with slightly torn/weathered edges and a second underlying poster layer peeking from beneath one corner.
street_poster.visual_hierarchy: Weathered top poster with a hint of a previous layer beneath.
street_poster.text_regions: Weathered caption on the top layer
street_poster.why_different: Shows a second poster layer beneath the visible one — a street-signage archaeology effect unlike any single-layer template.

billboard_mockup.concept: The photo is presented within an illustrated billboard structure (support poles, sky backdrop visible around it), viewed from a slight low angle.
billboard_mockup.visual_hierarchy: Billboard structure and environment frame the photo as a scene.
billboard_mockup.why_different: Places the photo within an illustrated environment (structure + sky + angle), not just a flat treatment on the photo itself.

fashion_zine.concept: A DIY punk-zine aesthetic — photocopied-texture grain, cut-and-paste mismatched-size headline lettering, and a staple-mark graphic in the corner.
fashion_zine.visual_hierarchy: Raw photocopy texture with ransom-note headline dominant.
fashion_zine.why_different: A DIY zine production language (ransom-note lettering + photocopy grain + staple) unlike any polished editorial device.

campaign_foldout.concept: Simulates a tri-fold foldout ad — two faint vertical fold-crease lines divide the canvas into three unequal panels, main photo in the centre panel.
campaign_foldout.visual_hierarchy: Three panels of unequal width, centre panel dominant.
campaign_foldout.why_different: Two fold creases creating three panels (a tri-fold), distinct from folio_spread's single two-page gutter fold.

runway_sequence.concept: Three small sequential thumbnail frames along the bottom edge suggest a runway walk sequence (approach/turn/exit), main photo large above them.
runway_sequence.visual_hierarchy: Large hero photo dominant; small sequence strip subordinate beneath.
runway_sequence.why_different: A highlight-reel/contact-strip hybrid using thumbnails (not full sprocket-strip frames like filmstrip_grid's decoration).

polaroid_wall.concept: Several small polaroid-style photo frames (white borders, blank caption strip) scatter/overlap at slight rotations behind or beside the main photo.
polaroid_wall.visual_hierarchy: Scattered polaroid objects at varied rotations around the main image.
polaroid_wall.image_regions: Main photo + several small polaroid insets
polaroid_wall.text_regions: Blank caption strips on each polaroid
polaroid_wall.why_different: Multiple overlapping polaroid-object frames at varied rotations — a physical scattered-photos collage illusion, distinct from any single-frame template.

editor_shortlist.concept: A small checklist of thumbnail alternates with one marked/circled as 'selected' sits beside the main photo, like an editor's contact-sheet pick.
editor_shortlist.visual_hierarchy: Main photo dominant; small rejected-alternates strip beside it.
editor_shortlist.why_different: Shows rejected alternates alongside the selected image (a curation-decision narrative), distinct from fashion_contact_sheet's broader proof board.

designer_notes.concept: Handwritten-style annotations specifically about fabric/construction details point to parts of a garment in the photo.
designer_notes.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant with construction-specific annotations pointing inward.
designer_notes.why_different: Annotations reference garment construction specifically, a fashion-design-process specificity distinct from editor_note's general editorial comment.

season_catalog.concept: A small seasonal icon (sun/snowflake/leaf) plus season name sits in a corner tag, catalog-style page number in the opposite corner.
season_catalog.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; small seasonal tag + page number as corner accents.
season_catalog.why_different: A seasonal icon system (weather/season symbol) as the categorisation device — distinct from any text-only season tag.

collection_board.concept: A moodboard-style arrangement of the main photo alongside 2-3 small colour/fabric swatch chips pinned around it, on a corkboard texture background.
collection_board.visual_hierarchy: Main photo with pinned swatch chips scattered around it on a textured backdrop.
collection_board.why_different: A corkboard/pinboard background with pinned elements — a physical pinboard illusion distinct from any flat swatch strip.

style_manifesto.concept: A single bold declarative sentence in extra-large italic serif dominates the composition, photo cropped small beneath it as supporting evidence.
style_manifesto.visual_hierarchy: Text dominates above the photo in scale — an inverted hierarchy.
style_manifesto.why_different: Text dominates over the photo in scale — an inverted hierarchy distinct from every photo-dominant template.

fashion_archive.concept: Styled like an archival fashion-house ledger page — faint ruled horizontal lines across the whole background, small archive reference code.
fashion_archive.visual_hierarchy: Full-canvas ledger-ruled background with photo and reference code overlaid.
fashion_archive.why_different: A full-canvas ledger-ruled-lines background — a bookkeeping/archive-paper texture distinct from any blank or textured field.

agency_board.concept: A modeling-agency comp-card layout — small headshot plus 3 tiny stat lines (placeholder measurement style) beside the main photo.
agency_board.visual_hierarchy: Main photo dominant; comp-card stat block subordinate.
agency_board.why_different: A comp-card/stat-line format (agency talent-sheet convention) — a talent-representation document style unlike any other template.

portfolio_case.concept: The photo appears to sit within an illustrated portfolio case/sleeve with a visible zipper or clasp graphic along one edge.
portfolio_case.why_different: A zipper/clasp hardware illustration — a functional-hardware decorative vocabulary not used elsewhere.

campaign_brief.concept: Styled like an internal brief document — small 'OBJECTIVE:', 'AUDIENCE:' field labels with short text sit in a side panel, photo the main visual.
campaign_brief.why_different: Internal strategy-document field labels — a business-document convention distinct from consultation_sheet's clinical intake form.

press_release.concept: Mimics a press-release header — 'FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE' small caps line at the top with a dateline, photo below as the accompanying image.
press_release.visual_hierarchy: Release header dominant at top; photo as supporting image beneath.
press_release.why_different: A specific press-release document header convention (dateline + release line) — a newsroom document simulation.

brand_manifest.concept: A numbered list of brand values/pillars ('1. Quality 2. Craft 3. Integrity') sits in a column beside the photo, sparse and declarative.
brand_manifest.visual_hierarchy: Photo and numbered values column as two zones.
brand_manifest.why_different: A numbered VALUES list (not a routine, checklist, or protocol) — a declarative brand-philosophy document distinct from any process-oriented list.

lookbook_story.concept: Two photos (main + one small inset) are connected by a thin dotted narrative line suggesting sequence/story progression, with a small 'Chapter 1' style tag.
lookbook_story.visual_hierarchy: Main photo and inset linked by a visible narrative connector line.
lookbook_story.why_different: Connects two images via a narrative-sequence line with chapter tagging — a two-image story-linking device distinct from any single-image template.

editorial_stack.concept: 3-4 thin horizontal caption strips of decreasing width stack like a printed masthead hierarchy (headline/subhead/kicker/byline) above the photo.
editorial_stack.visual_hierarchy: Full typographic hierarchy stack above the photo.
editorial_stack.why_different: A complete masthead hierarchy (4 distinct text tiers of decreasing prominence) — more elaborate than any single-headline template.

fashion_banner.concept: A slim horizontal banner strip crosses the photo with a repeating marquee-style visual pattern (same word repeating), like a runway show banner.
fashion_banner.visual_hierarchy: Photo full-bleed; repeating marquee strip crosses it.
fashion_banner.text_regions: Repeated word/phrase in the marquee strip
fashion_banner.why_different: A repeated marquee/ticker-style text pattern — a rhythmic repetition device unlike any single-instance caption.

ios_widget.concept: The photo sits within a rounded-corner iOS-style widget card with a small app-icon-style badge and subtle inner shadow, mimicking a home-screen widget.
ios_widget.visual_hierarchy: Widget card frame dominant, photo fills it.
ios_widget.text_regions: Small caption on the widget card
ios_widget.why_different: Mimics a specific OS widget convention (rounded corners + app badge + inner shadow) — a recognisable platform-specific UI reference.

android_cards.concept: Material-Design-style elevated card with a coloured accent strip along one edge and a floating action button (FAB) circle graphic in the corner.
android_cards.visual_hierarchy: Elevated card frame with a FAB accent.
android_cards.why_different: Material Design elevation + FAB circle — a distinct OS design-language reference (Android) from ios_widget's iOS convention.

dashboard_clean.concept: A minimal single-stat dashboard tile (big number, small label, thin sparkline) sits beside the photo.
dashboard_clean.visual_hierarchy: Photo and single-stat tile as two zones.
dashboard_clean.text_regions: Big number + label on the stat tile
dashboard_clean.why_different: Includes a sparkline (tiny trend graph) — a data-trend visualisation element not used in any other template.

analytics_story.concept: A vertical Instagram-story-style segmented progress bar sits at the very top (like story viewing progress dots), photo below.
analytics_story.visual_hierarchy: Progress-dot bar at top, photo dominant below.
analytics_story.why_different: A segmented progress-bar (social-app story UI convention) — a platform-chrome reference distinct from any print-based device.

device_frame.concept: The photo is shown inset within an illustrated smartphone device frame (rounded bezel, notch, side buttons), like a screenshot mockup.
device_frame.visual_hierarchy: Device bezel frame dominant, photo as the 'screen'.
device_frame.why_different: A full physical device bezel illustration (buttons, notch) — the most literal hardware-mockup device in the set.

browser_tabs.concept: Small browser tab shapes with tiny favicon dots sit along the top edge like open browser tabs, photo fills the 'page' below.
browser_tabs.visual_hierarchy: Tab row at top, photo as the page content below.
browser_tabs.text_regions: Small text in the active tab
browser_tabs.why_different: Multiple tab shapes implying several open pages — a multi-tab browser-chrome reference distinct from a single window frame.

desktop_cards.concept: Several small floating rounded cards of varying size scatter loosely across a desktop-style backdrop, main photo the largest card.
desktop_cards.visual_hierarchy: Loosely scattered cards of varying size, largest is the photo.
desktop_cards.why_different: Loose scattered cards of varying size (not aligned/stacked) — a casual desktop-clutter arrangement distinct from any aligned grid or stack.

command_bar.concept: A search/command-palette bar (rounded pill shape with a cursor-blink graphic and placeholder text) floats centred over the photo.
command_bar.visual_hierarchy: Command bar as a centred floating accent over the full-bleed photo.
command_bar.text_regions: Placeholder text in the command bar
command_bar.why_different: Includes a blinking-cursor detail implying live/active input — a micro-interaction illusion unique to this template.

floating_panels.concept: 2-3 translucent glass panels of different sizes float at slightly different implied depths (via blur/shadow intensity) over the photo.
floating_panels.visual_hierarchy: Multiple panels layered at different depths over the photo.
floating_panels.why_different: Multiple panels at different implied depths (varying blur amounts) — a layered-depth illusion distinct from a single flat glass panel.

timeline_cards.concept: A small vertical timeline with dot markers and a connecting line runs down one side, each dot linked to a mini caption card.
timeline_cards.visual_hierarchy: Vertical timeline with linked caption cards down one side.
timeline_cards.text_regions: Mini cards at each timeline dot
timeline_cards.why_different: A vertical timeline with connected dot-markers (chronological UI convention) — distinct from treatment_journey's horizontal milestone path.

search_results.concept: Mimics a search-results list — photo as the top 'result' with a small URL-style breadcrumb line and 2 muted 'related result' placeholder lines below.
search_results.visual_hierarchy: Top result (photo) dominant, muted related results beneath.
search_results.why_different: Shows related/alternate results beneath the main one (implying a search context) — a discovery/browsing narrative distinct from any single-focus template.

profile_layout.concept: A circular avatar crop plus a small stat row (placeholder counter numbers) sits above the main photo grid, like a social profile header.
profile_layout.visual_hierarchy: Profile header (avatar + stats) above the main content grid.
profile_layout.why_different: Includes a stat row (multiple small numeric counters side by side) — distinct from testimonial_card's avatar+quote focus with no stats.

app_showcase.concept: The photo is displayed within a rounded app-icon-shaped mask (superellipse/squircle), small app name label beneath.
app_showcase.why_different: A squircle (superellipse) mask shape — a specific geometric mask distinct from circle, rectangle, or hexagon.

widget_columns.concept: Three narrow equal-width widget columns of different content types (photo, stat, caption) sit side by side like a widget dashboard row.
widget_columns.why_different: Three distinct simultaneous content types in parallel columns — a true multi-widget dashboard row, more complex than any two-zone split.

quick_actions.concept: A row of small rounded action-button chips (icon + label) sits beneath the photo like a UI quick-actions bar.
quick_actions.why_different: A row of discrete actionable-looking button chips implies interactivity/choice — a UI-affordance device unlike any static label.

status_board.concept: A kanban-style board with 2-3 small labelled columns ('To Book' / 'In Progress' / 'Complete') with the photo as a card within one column.
status_board.visual_hierarchy: Full kanban board structure, photo as one card within it.
status_board.why_different: A full kanban board structure with the photo as a card inside one column — a project-management board metaphor entirely new to the set.

notification_feed.concept: 2-3 stacked notification-style banners (rounded rectangles with small icon + timestamp) overlay the top of the photo like a phone lock-screen.
notification_feed.visual_hierarchy: Stacked notification banners over the top of the full-bleed photo.
notification_feed.why_different: Includes timestamps on each notification (implying real-time sequence) — a lock-screen stack, temporally specific unlike any static card.

calendar_cards.concept: A small month-calendar grid with one date circled sits beside the photo, appointment-booking style.
calendar_cards.visual_hierarchy: Photo and month-calendar grid as two zones.
calendar_cards.why_different: An actual calendar month grid (not just a week strip like routine_calendar) — a full monthly date-picker reference.

insight_panel.concept: A single 'insight' callout card with a lightbulb-style icon and one short tip sentence sits over a corner of the photo.
insight_panel.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; small insight callout as a corner accent.
insight_panel.text_regions: Short tip sentence in the callout
insight_panel.why_different: A single-tip callout with an icon specifically implying an insight/idea moment — a micro-content format distinct from any longer text panel.

metric_dashboard.concept: 3 small circular progress-ring gauges (like fitness-app rings) sit in a row beneath the photo, each representing a different metric.
metric_dashboard.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; ring-gauge row beneath as a metric summary.
metric_dashboard.why_different: Multiple circular progress rings (not bars, not sparklines) — a specific fitness-app gauge convention, visually distinct from any bar or numeric stat.

pressed_leaves_collection.concept: Several individually rendered pressed-leaf silhouettes (varied botanical shapes) scatter across one corner of the photo, overlapping slightly.
pressed_leaves_collection.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; scattered leaf collection as a corner accent.
pressed_leaves_collection.why_different: Multiple distinct leaf silhouettes scattered (a naturalist collection), richer than pressed_botanical's single specimen + vellum texture.

stone_layers.concept: The background shows stacked/layered flat stone slabs (like a zen rock stack) viewed from the side, photo inset as if resting atop the stack.
stone_layers.visual_hierarchy: Balanced stone-stack illustration supporting the inset photo.
stone_layers.why_different: A stacked/balanced rock-cairn implying physical equilibrium — a sculptural object, not a flat material texture like stone_texture.

wood_grain.concept: Fine wood-grain texture with visible growth rings overlays the background field, with a thin darker 'knot' detail placed off-centre.
wood_grain.visual_hierarchy: Photo with wood-texture field; knot as a focal accent.
wood_grain.why_different: Includes a wood knot detail (an organic irregularity marker) as a focal accent — distinct from any uniform texture field.

linen_fold_dimensional.concept: Linen fabric texture is shown with a visible three-dimensional fold creating an actual raised fold silhouette across the frame.
linen_fold_dimensional.visual_hierarchy: Raised 3D fold silhouette crosses the composition.
linen_fold_dimensional.text_regions: In the flatter zone beside the fold
decorative_elements: raised 3D fold silhouette with light/shadow modelling
linen_fold_dimensional.why_different: A raised 3D fold silhouette (light/shadow modelling a physical ridge), more dimensional than linen_press_fold's flat crease shadow.

organic_shadow.concept: An irregular, organic-shaped soft shadow (like a plant's shadow) falls across part of the photo, implying an off-frame light source and object.
organic_shadow.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; irregular organic shadow as atmosphere.
organic_shadow.why_different: An irregular organic shadow shape (implying an off-frame plant/object) rather than a geometric or architectural shadow.

sunlight_window.concept: A window-pane-shaped light patch (with visible mullion cross-shadow) falls across the photo as if sunlight streams through a nearby window.
sunlight_window.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; window-light patch as atmosphere.
sunlight_window.why_different: A specific window-mullion cross-shadow light pattern (a precise light-source geometry) rather than generic shadow_play.

glass_reflection.concept: A subtle diagonal glass-reflection streak (light glare band) crosses the photo, as if viewed through glass.
glass_reflection.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; diagonal glare streak as atmosphere.
glass_reflection.why_different: A diagonal glare/reflection streak simulating viewing-through-glass (a shop window), distinct from glass_dashboard's UI glassmorphism panel.

botanical_curve.concept: A single elegant curved botanical stem/vine line traces across the composition from corner to corner, minimal and linear.
botanical_curve.visual_hierarchy: One continuous curved vine line crossing the frame.
botanical_curve.why_different: One continuous curved vine line (a linear botanical gesture) versus pressed_leaves_collection's scattered discrete shapes.

marble_plate.concept: A solid marble-texture plate/slab sits as a distinct panel beside the photo, full material presentation.
marble_plate.visual_hierarchy: Photo and marble panel as two equal-weight zones.
marble_plate.why_different: A full solid marble panel (equal-weight zone) rather than marble_vein_inlay's thin edge-line accent.

ceramic_tile.concept: A repeating small ceramic-tile grid pattern (grout lines visible) borders one edge of the photo, with handmade glaze-variation texture per tile.
ceramic_tile.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; tile-grid border along one edge.
decorative_elements: repeating tile grid with grout lines
ceramic_tile.why_different: A repeating tile-grid with grout lines and per-tile glaze variation — a handmade-tile material reference distinct from ceramic_frame's border-only concept.

fabric_layers.concept: 2-3 different fabric textures (linen, silk, wool swatches) are layered with slight overlap and offset, like a textile mood-board stack.
fabric_layers.visual_hierarchy: Layered fabric swatches as a compositional stack.
fabric_layers.why_different: Multiple distinct fabric types layered together (a textile combination), richer than any single-texture template.

cotton_texture.concept: Soft cotton-fibre texture with visible fluffy fibre edges frames the photo's border organically.
cotton_texture.visual_hierarchy: Photo with a soft fluffy-textured border.
cotton_texture.why_different: A soft fluffy fibre-edge texture (cotton) versus letterpress_deboss_card's rough torn-paper deckle edge — a different material entirely.

earth_tones.concept: The composition is built around a warm earth-tone gradient wash (terracotta-to-sand) with a thin horizon-line graphic suggesting a landscape.
earth_tones.visual_hierarchy: Gradient wash field with a horizon line as the sole structural anchor.
earth_tones.text_regions: Above or below the horizon line
earth_tones.why_different: Includes a horizon line (landscape reference) — a geographic/environmental compositional anchor unlike any material swatch.

natural_frame.concept: The photo is bordered by an illustrated arrangement of small natural objects (pebbles, twigs) forming an organic irregular frame edge.
natural_frame.visual_hierarchy: Photo bordered by an assembled ring of natural objects.
natural_frame.why_different: A frame made of discrete object illustrations (pebbles/twigs placed individually) rather than a continuous texture or line — an assembled-object border.

shadow_grid.concept: A grid of small geometric shadow blocks (like light through a lattice/screen) falls across the photo in a repeating pattern.
shadow_grid.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; repeating lattice-shadow pattern as atmosphere.
shadow_grid.text_regions: Away from the densest shadow area
shadow_grid.why_different: A repeating grid of shadow shapes (lattice-light pattern) — distinct from sunlight_window's single window-pane shadow.

clay_pattern.concept: A hand-sculpted clay-relief pattern (organic bumps/ridges, like unglazed pottery) textures a border band around the photo.
clay_pattern.visual_hierarchy: Photo centred within a sculpted-relief border band.
clay_pattern.why_different: A sculpted relief pattern (dimensional bumps, not flat colour) — a tactile ceramic-craft texture distinct from ceramic_tile's flat glazed grid.

sand_curves.concept: Soft flowing sand-dune-style curved lines (like wind-rippled sand) trace across the lower portion of the photo.
sand_curves.visual_hierarchy: Photo dominant; dune curves as a lower-third accent.
sand_curves.text_regions: Upper area, away from the curves
sand_curves.why_different: Wind-rippled dune curves (a specific natural erosion pattern) — distinct from water_surface's reflective-water concept.

water_surface.concept: The photo's lower portion shows a reflective water-surface effect with a soft mirrored reflection of the upper image.
water_surface.visual_hierarchy: Upper photo mirrored softly in a lower water-reflection zone.
water_surface.why_different: An actual mirrored reflection of the photo itself (not just a ripple texture) — a reflection-doubling illusion unique to this template.

leaf_overlay.concept: A single large leaf silhouette (translucent, oversized) overlays across the entire photo like a natural vignette/veil.
leaf_overlay.visual_hierarchy: Oversized translucent leaf veils most of the frame.
leaf_overlay.text_regions: In the clearer negative space of the leaf shape
leaf_overlay.why_different: One oversized translucent leaf covering most of the frame (a veil effect) — distinct in scale from pressed_leaves_collection's small scattered collection.

moss_texture.concept: Soft, dense moss-green micro-texture covers a border band around the photo, with tiny highlight dots suggesting dew.
moss_texture.visual_hierarchy: Photo centred within a dewy moss-textured border.
moss_texture.why_different: Includes dew-drop highlight details on the moss — a micro-detail (moisture) unlike any dry material texture in the set.

cutout_overlap.concept: Two photo crops overlap each other at an angle with a hard-edge overlap boundary, like layered cut-paper shapes with no gradient blend.
cutout_overlap.visual_hierarchy: Two overlapping crops, hard boundary where they meet.
cutout_overlap.text_regions: In the negative space beside the overlap
cutout_overlap.why_different: Two overlapping photo crops with a hard edge boundary — a paper-cutout collage algorithm distinct from any single-photo template.

floating_ribbons.concept: 2-3 curved ribbon-shaped image crops (bent rectangles, not straight) float across the canvas at different angles.
floating_ribbons.visual_hierarchy: Multiple curved image-ribbons floating at varied angles.
floating_ribbons.why_different: Curved (non-rectilinear) image-crop ribbons — a bent-shape masking algorithm unlike any straight-edge crop.

angled_panels.concept: 3-4 photo/colour panels are arranged at varied non-90° angles (like scattered cards on a table), overlapping slightly.
angled_panels.why_different: Multiple panels at independent non-orthogonal angles (not a single rotation) — a scattered-table arrangement algorithm.

wave_columns.concept: Vertical column dividers follow a gentle sine-wave curve instead of straight lines, photo clipped between two wave-curves.
wave_columns.visual_hierarchy: Photo confined between two curved wave-boundaries.
wave_columns.why_different: Sine-wave curved column boundaries (mathematical curve dividers) — a unique non-linear division algorithm.

circular_story.concept: Content is arranged along a circular path — caption text curves around a circle — photo centred within the circle.
circular_story.visual_hierarchy: Circular text path surrounding a centred photo.
circular_story.why_different: Text that follows a circular curve (not straight lines) — a typographic-path algorithm distinct from any linear caption.

ring_focus.concept: A thin ring outline surrounds a specific focal detail in the photo (not the whole photo), like a magnifying halo, rest of frame normal.
ring_focus.visual_hierarchy: Localized ring highlight on one detail within an otherwise normal photo.
ring_focus.image_regions: Full photo, with one ringed detail
ring_focus.why_different: The ring highlights a specific detail within the photo (not the whole frame) — a localized-focus algorithm distinct from loupe_inspection's separate zoomed inset.

arc_sequence.concept: 3-4 small content elements are arranged along a curved arc path (not a straight line or full circle), like beads on a curved wire.
arc_sequence.visual_hierarchy: Elements placed along a partial arc, largest at one end.
arc_sequence.why_different: A partial arc (not full circle, not straight line) placement path — a distinct intermediate curve geometry.

diagonal_crop.concept: The photo is cropped by a single bold diagonal line into two unequal triangular zones, one photo, one solid colour.
diagonal_crop.visual_hierarchy: Two triangular zones split by one diagonal line.
diagonal_crop.why_different: A diagonal (not vertical/horizontal) hard split into triangular zones — a different cut angle than any existing split template.

offset_window.concept: A rectangular 'window' crop of the photo is deliberately offset/shifted off-centre, overlapping and bleeding off the canvas edge; rest is solid colour.
offset_window.visual_hierarchy: Off-centre photo window partially cropped by the canvas edge.
offset_window.why_different: The photo window deliberately bleeds off the canvas edge on one side — an intentionally incomplete-window algorithm.
